summary: Read-only R3 native instruction authority review. Inspected MEMORY.md, OS remaining gaps plan, completion conditions, AGENTS protocol, native instruction authority scanner/verifier, health status, closed-loop goal gate, Observatory exposure, current native authority status artifact, and local Codex/Claude instruction surface listings. Verdict is REVISE_REQUIRED because current docs/code cover user-over-memory authority and many native surfaces, but do not explicitly require scanning user-level Codex rules/custom policy files such as <machine-local-path> which is safe-readable and currently outside the scanner surface list.
decisions: Current user instruction > OS memory is documented in AGENTS.md and OS completion docs, and enforced by native-authority regression/current checks in verify:goal.

P0-05 in the remaining-gaps plan requires scanning AGENTS, repo .codex, Codex global config/hooks, Claude settings, installer-written hooks, and emitting native_memory_drift/wrong_memory_reference_detected/unsafe_native_instruction/hook_authority_conflict signals.

The implementation scans many required surfaces and the current data artifact reports healthy, but it omits <machine-local-path> a safe-readable native Codex policy/custom rules surface that could drift without tripping native_instruction_authority.
next_steps: Revise the plan/completion docs to explicitly include Codex user-level rules/custom policy files such as ~/.codex/rules/*.rules and any equivalent Claude user-level custom instruction files when present.
Require status:native-authority/verify:goal to fail when a safe-readable native instruction/custom policy surface exists but is not scanned or explicitly classified out with a reason.
